revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Fukami-jinja]][[ja:深見神社]] {{Expand Japanese|深見神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Fukami Shrine | native_name = 深見神社 | image = Fukami-jinja-haiden.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Fukami Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.468777|139.471665}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Kuraokami|lt=Kuraokami|de|Kuraokami|en|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|sv|Okami-no-Kami|zh|闇淤加美神|qid=Q644201|1=Kuraokami}} }} {{nihongo|'''Fukami Shrine'''|深見神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Sagami Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11582137|ltq=Q11582137|qid=Q11582137|1=List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province}} ** Series ordinal: 9 ** P155: {{ill|Ohohano Shrine|lt=Ohohano Shrine|qid=Q135039722|1=Ohohano Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q17217329|ltq=Q17217329|qid=Q17217329|1=Utsumochi Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Yamato, Kanagawa|lt=Yamato|ar|ياماتو (كاناغاوا)|azb|یاماتو، کاناقاوا|be|Ямата (горад)|bg|Ямато (град)|ca|Yamato (Kanagawa)|ceb|Yamato-shi|ce|Ямато (гӀала)|cs|Jamato (Kanagawa)|de|Yamato (Kanagawa)|en|Yamato, Kanagawa|eo|Jamato (Kanagavo)|es|Yamato (Kanagawa)|et|Yamato (Kanagawa)|fa|یاماتو، کاناگاوا|fi|Yamato (Kanagawa)|fr|Yamato (Kanagawa)|ga|Yamato|ie|Yamato (Kanagawa)|it|Yamato (Kanagawa)|ja|大和市|javoyage|大和市|ko|야마토시|mzn|یاماتو، کاناگاوا|nl|Yamato (Kanagawa)|no|Yamato (Kanagawa)|pl|Yamato (Kanagawa)|pt|Yamato (Kanagawa)|ro|Yamato, Kanagawa|ru|Ямато (город)|sh|Jamato|simple|Yamato, Kanagawa|sr|Јамато (град)|sv|Yamato, Kanagawa|tg|Ямато|th|ยามาโตะ (จังหวัดคานางาวะ)|tl|Yamato, Kanagawa|tr|Yamato, Kanagawa|tt|Ямато (шәһәр)|uk|Ямато (Канаґава)|ur|یماٹو، کناگوا|uz|Yamato (Kanagawa)|vi|Yamato, Kanagawa|war|Yamato, Kanagawa|zh_classical|大和市|zh_min_nan|Yamato-chhī|zh_yue|大和市|zh|大和市|qid=Q386697|1=Yamato, Kanagawa}} * {{ill|Q1053732|ltq=Q1053732|qid=Q1053732|1=Sagami Province}} * {{ill|Q1202776|ltq=Q1202776|qid=Q1202776|1=Kōza District, Kanagawa}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.468777|139.471665}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Fukami-jinja-haiden.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q644201|ltq=Q644201|qid=Q644201|1=Kuraokami}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181684 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|es|Shinmeizukuri|fr|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|ru|Симмэй-дзукури|tr|Shinmei-zukuri|zh|神明造|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9021005004884 == Commons category (P373) == * Fukami-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '神奈川県大和市深見3367', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Fukami-jinja}} [[JA:深見神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11560278}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Fukami Shrine |画像= [[File: yamato city fukami jinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|28|7|N|139|28|17.8|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|qid=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamato, Kanagawa|Q386697|qid=Q386697|1=Yamato, Kanagawa}} 3367 Fukami |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Okami|Q644201|qid=Q644201|lt=Kuraokami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}}{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 深見村 鹿島社}}
See also {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=#Enshrined deities|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) 22nd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|qid=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} ({{ill|lt=478|Q30910|qid=Q30910|1=478}}) ('Sōkoku Fudoki') |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = Kashima Shrine (Kashima-sama) |例祭 = September, the day before {{ill|lt=Respect for the Aged Day|Q1186127|qid=Q1186127|1=Respect for the Aged Day}}From the official pamphlet 'Engishiki Fukami Shrine History.' In the past, it was held on the fixed date of {{ill|lt=September 15|Q2846|qid=Q2846|1=September 15}}.
See also {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=#Rituals|1=UNKNOWN}} |緯度度=35|緯度分=28|緯度秒=07.0|N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N |経度度=139|経度分=28|経度秒=17.8|E(東経)及びW(西経)=E |地図国コード=JP |地図=Japan Kanagawa |ラベル位置=bottom }} [[File:Fukami-jinja-torii(west).JPG|thumb|250px|right|Torii on the west side (photographed January 2013)]] '''Fukami Shrine''' (ふかみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Fukami, {{ill|lt=Yamato, Kanagawa|Q386697|qid=Q386697|1=Yamato, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|qid=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is regarded as one of the thirteen shrines listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' (AD {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}, {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5) for {{ill|lt=Sagami Province|Q1053732|qid=Q1053732|1=Sagami Province}} (a minor shrine){{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 深見村 鹿島社}}. Also, in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}} gazetteer '{{ill|lt=Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudokikō|Q11503942|qid=Q11503942|1=Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudoki-kō}},' it is written as '''Kashima Shrine'''{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 深見村 鹿島社}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Okami|Q644201|qid=Q644201|lt=Kuraokami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}} (Kuraokami; rain deity) *: Considered the original enshrined deity at the time of this shrine’s founding. In {{ill|lt=2012|Q1990|qid=Q1990|1=2012}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 24), it was merged from the precinct shrine Okura {{ill|lt=Inari shrine|Q514480|qid=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}}. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} (Takemikazuchi; deity of enduring martial fortune) *: Said to have been {{ill|lt=kanjō|Q195793|qid=Q195793|lt=divided and invited|1=Bunrei}} from {{ill|lt=Kashima Jingū|Q706499|qid=Q706499|1=Kashima Jingū}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|qid=Q1055353|1=Hitachi Province}} by the Sakamoto family, lords of {{ill|lt=Fukami Domain|Q10863506|qid=Q10863506|1=Fukami Domain}} in the early Edo period, and has since been treated as this shrine’s deity. At that time, Kuraokami, formerly the deity of this shrine, is thought to have been merged into the precinct shrine Okura Inari. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}} (Takeminakata) *: In {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|qid=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42), merged on equal footing with this shrine’s deities from the extra-precinct {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrine|Q135009906|qid=Q135009906|1=Massha}} {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine|Q2370082|qid=Q2370082|1=Suwa Shrine}} in Fukami. :''For transitions in this shrine’s enshrined deities, see the later section “{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=#Changes in enshrined deities and origin of the alternate name “Kashima Shrine”|1=UNKNOWN}}.”'' === Origins and history === A fire in {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|qid=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9)From the “History” board in Fukami Shrine precincts and the official pamphlet 'Engishiki Fukami Shrine History.' Some sources, including the Kanagawa Association of Shrines website, say the fire occurred in “Meiji 8” ({{ill|lt=1875|Q7764|qid=Q7764|1=1875}}). destroyed everything from shrine buildings to {{ill|lt=ancient documents|Q89298567|qid=Q89298567|1=komonjo}}. The following is the tradition transmitted at this shrine, but except for matters recorded in historical {{ill|lt=historical literature|Q3771738|qid=Q3771738|1=historical document}} such as the 'Sōkoku Fudoki' and the 'Engishiki Jinmyōchō,' it does not survive today as formal {{ill|lt=documents|Q49848|qid=Q49848|1=Document}} ({{ill|lt=historical sources|Q3750478|qid=Q3750478|1=Historical source}}). === Beginnings of Fukami and this shrine (documents and shrine tradition) === According to the 'Sōkoku Fudoki,' it was “first enshrined in the 3rd month of the 22nd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|qid=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} ({{ill|lt=478|Q30910|qid=Q30910|1=478}}),” but the exact founding date is unknown'Kanagawa Shrines Guidebook: A Stroll of the Heart' (Kanashin Publishing / supervised by Kanagawa Association of Shrines, Oct. 20, 1997), p.92. It also states that the deity enshrined then was “Kuraokami”. On the other hand, before Takeminakata was merged in the Meiji period, Kuraokami had, through some process, been removed from the main hall’s deity lineup and became a deity of the precinct shrine Okura Inari; until that merger, only Takemikazuchi was the deity of this shrine (main hall) (for the currently prominent explanation of this process, see {{ill|lt=#Changes in enshrined deities and origin of the alternate name “Kashima Shrine”|UNKNOWN|lt=below}}). According to the theory of prefectural historic-site investigator Akira Ishino, the sea of {{ill|lt=Sagami Bay|Q1061221|qid=Q1061221|1=Sagami Bay}} once extended deeply into this area, and transportation by {{ill|lt=boats|Q4265275|qid=Q4265275|1=boat}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=rafts|1=UNKNOWN}} was established (this {{ill|lt=inlet|Q1172599|qid=Q1172599|1=Inlet}} is provisionally called the “Old Fukami Inlet”). Furthermore, as a collective name for the whole {{ill|lt=Sakaigawa River (Tokyo/Kanagawa)|Q6375268|qid=Q6375268|lt=Sakaigawa|1=Sakai River (Tokyo, Kanagawa)}} basin, Fukami was formerly written as “Deep Sea” or “Deep Water.” The place name Fukami first appears in literature in the '{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|qid=Q2702542|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}},' compiled in the {{ill|lt=Jōhei|Q1324874|qid=Q1324874|lt=Jōhei|1=Jōhei}} era ({{ill|lt=931|Q24789|qid=Q24789|1=931}}–{{ill|lt=938|Q24794|qid=Q24794|1=938}}), where it is written as “Fukami-go, {{ill|lt=Kōza District|Q1202776|qid=Q1202776|1=Kōza District, Kanagawa}}, Sagami Province”; here too it denotes a broader area than today’s locality. In this shrine’s {{ill|lt=engi|Q522046|qid=Q522046|1=Pratītyasamutpāda}} (origin account), it is said that when Takemikazuchi (this shrine’s deity), whose purpose was pacification of the {{ill|lt=eastern provinces|Q10343838|qid=Q10343838|1=Tōgoku}}, advanced from Kashima in {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|qid=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi|1=Hitachi Province}} with a naval force ({{ill|lt=navy|Q11129885|qid=Q11129885|lt=boat corps|1=boat corps}}) into “deep sea,” the land was governed by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi|1=UNKNOWN}}’s child {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}} and Kuraokami. Kuraokami, the rain deity, opened fertile rice fields and founded a settlement by local people. This is said to be the beginning of Fukami (both deities had been enshrined at Okura Inari in the precincts, but after Kuraokami was merged into the main hall, only Ukanomitama remains there today). === Worship of this shrine === It has long been the center of worship for local people, and was particularly devoutly revered by figures including {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|qid=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Later Hōjō clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|qid=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}}, {{ill|lt=Shibuya Shigekuni|Q11561779|qid=Q11561779|lt=Shibuya no Shōji Shigekuni|1=Shibuya Shigekuni}}, and {{ill|lt=Ōta Dōkan|Q386144|qid=Q386144|1=Ōta Dōkan}}. During the {{ill|lt=Siege of Osaka|Q475780|qid=Q475780|1=Siege of Osaka}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|1=UNKNOWN}}, the {{ill|lt=hatamoto|Q572662|qid=Q572662|1=Hatamoto}} {{ill|lt=Sakamoto Shigeyasu|Q11425380|qid=Q11425380|lt=Sakamoto Kozayemon Shigeyasu|1=Sakamoto Shigeyasu}}, who prayed here for lasting martial fortune, also donated {{ill|lt=rice fields|Q842623|qid=Q842623|1=Paddy field}} at that time, and these remain as “Kashimada.” Furthermore, {{ill|lt=Sakamoto Shigeharu|Q11425381|qid=Q11425381|1=Sakamoto Shigeharu}}, Shigeyasu’s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=adopted son|1=UNKNOWN}} and later {{ill|lt=Commissioner of Temples and Shrines|Q1036279|qid=Q1036279|1=Jisha-bugyō}}, is said to have repeatedly worshiped here, overseen construction of shrine buildings, and erected a shrine name marker inscribed “Sagami no Kuni, one of the thirteen seats: Fukami Shrine”. The Sakamoto family were then the lords of Fukami. === Since modern times === In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), by {{ill|lt=Daijō-kan proclamations and directives|Q11444008|qid=Q11444008|lt=Daijō-kan proclamation|1=Dajōkan Edict}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. However, three years later in {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|qid=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), it was devastated by a fire from neighboring {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Butsudō-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}; everything from shrine buildings to old documents burned, and it fell into ruin, with even its official rank becoming unclear. In {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|qid=Q2057|1=1909}} (Meiji 42), it merged with the subsidiary Suwa Shrine that stood in Fukami’s Suwa-no-Mori, and Takeminakata was merged into this shrine on equal footing (aiden). At this stage there was only a temporary shrine building (kariden), with no main hall etc. built. Thereafter, recovery plans were made repeatedly but not realized; only in {{ill|lt=1941|Q5231|qid=Q5231|1=1941}} (Shōwa 16), 66 years after the fire, was it finally rebuilt at its current location (including the shrine buildings, {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|qid=Q234731|1=Torii}}, and subsidiary shrines). The following year it was again ranked as a gōsha (the {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former rank system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was abolished in {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|qid=Q18610|1=1946}} (Shōwa 21)){{R|"chosa"|"jinjyacho"}}. In 2012 (Heisei 24), to commemorate the 70th anniversary of reconstruction, a new shrine name marker was erected, and Kuraokami—the deity listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'—was merged from Okura Inari into the main hall. === Affiliated and subsidiary shrines === === Shrines within the precincts === [[File:Fukami-jinja-okura-to-yasukuni.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Okura Inari and Yasukuni-sha standing side by side]] [[File:Fukami-jinja-torii(east).JPG|thumb|250px|right|Torii on the east side: said to be the original torii, and the old shrine buildings are thought to have stood near Okura Inari in a straight line from here]] Okura Inari Shrine and Yasukuni-sha are enshrined on the west side of this shrine’s {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|qid=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} in an east-facing row. * Okura Inari Shrine (Okura Inari) *: {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}} is enshrined. Until 2012 (Heisei 24), Kuraokami—now a deity of the main hall—was also enshrined there. It is said to be the old shrine site of Fukami Shrine, and the haiden is said to have once stood near the Inari facing east (currently south-facing). *: Note that shrine buildings at the current location were only constructed in 1941 (Shōwa 16)[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304141429/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki02/sagami/bun/sag160502-01.html Fukami Shrine] (Survey of Engishiki Shrines). * {{ill|lt=Kōkū Shrine|Q30319396|qid=Q30319396|lt=Yasukuni-sha|1=Aviation Shrines}} (Atsugi Air Shrine, {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|qid=Q135009904|1=Sessha}}) *: Founded in November {{ill|lt=1944|Q5268|qid=Q5268|1=1944}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 19) as “Atsugi Air Shrine” within the grounds of {{ill|lt=Atsugi Naval Airfield|Q3016710|qid=Q3016710|1=Naval Air Facility Atsugi}}, enshrining war dead of the {{ill|lt=302nd Naval Air Group|Q11602156|qid=Q11602156|lt=Atsugi Air Group|1=302nd Naval Air Group}} in the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|qid=Q184425|1=Pacific War}}. After the war, when abolition was ordered, it was transferred on April 7, {{ill|lt=1951|Q18591|qid=Q18591|1=1951}} (Shōwa 26) to this shrine’s grounds, where local Fukami war dead were also merged[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=660&cd=1207077&scd=&npg=0 Fukami Shrine] (Kanagawa Association of Shrines). * Tomizawa Inari Shrine *: Strictly speaking, it cannot be directly reached from inside this shrine’s precincts (blocked by trees), but it stands at the southwest corner of the precinct area. The entrance is just south after passing this shrine’s west torii. It is thought to be an Inari shrine connected with the Tomizawa family, who established a {{ill|lt=silk-reeling industry|Q7515235|qid=Q7515235|lt=silk reeling|1=Silk throwing}} company in Fukami[http://53020342.at.webry.info/201010/article_13.html A journey beyond time and space to “Fukami Domain”] (Osanpo Seikatsu). File:Fukami-jinja-okura.JPG|
Okura Inari Shrine
File:Fukami-jinja-yasukuni.JPG|
Yasukuni-sha (Atsugi Air Shrine)
 === Shrines outside the precincts === * {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|Q2599651|qid=Q2599651|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)}} ({{ill|lt=subsidiary shrine|Q135009906|qid=Q135009906|1=Massha}}) - Located at 3015 Fukami, Yamato City (about 5 minutes’ walk north from this shrine). * {{ill|lt=Yakumo Shrine|Q11391847|qid=Q11391847|1=Yakumo shrine}} (subsidiary shrine) - Located at 481 Fukami, Yamato City (about 35 minutes’ walk north from this shrine). === Rituals === The traditional fixed dates for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=rituals|1=UNKNOWN}} are listed below. * {{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|qid=Q2150|1=January 1}} - {{ill|lt=Saitan-sai|Q11546824|qid=Q11546824|1=Saitan-sai}} * {{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|qid=Q2315|1=February 3}} - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Setsubun Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=September 15|Q2846|qid=Q2846|1=September 15}} - '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Annual Grand Festival|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (currently held the day before {{ill|lt=Respect for the Aged Day|Q1186127|qid=Q1186127|1=Respect for the Aged Day}}) === Changes in enshrined deities and origin of the alternate name “Kashima Shrine” === The 'Sōkoku Fudoki' says, “Enshrined in the 3rd month of the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku ({{ill|lt=478|Q30910|qid=Q30910|1=478}}), and the deity is Kuraokami,” and the original deity is considered to have been Kuraokami. On the other hand, the alternate name “Kashima Shrine” is said to come from the Sakamoto family, former local lords, having {{ill|lt=kanjō|Q195793|qid=Q195793|lt=invited a divided spirit|1=Bunrei}} of the deity of {{ill|lt=Kashima Jingū|Q706499|qid=Q706499|1=Kashima Jingū}} (now in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}) to this shrine. Takemikazuchi, one of this shrine’s deities, is said to have been enshrined at that time[http://www.yamato-kankou.com/%E3%82%84%E3%81%BE%E3%81%A8%E3%81%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Shrines of Yamato] (Yamato City Event & Tourism Association), and the Kanagawa Association of Shrines / Kanagawa Shimbun 'Kanagawa Shrines Guidebook' explains that “after entering the Edo period, {{ill|lt=Sakamoto Shigeyasu|Q11425380|qid=Q11425380|lt=Sakamoto Kozayemon Shigeyasu|1=Sakamoto Shigeyasu}} installed Takemikazuchi, a divided spirit of Kashima Jingū, as the enshrined deity[http://members.jcom.home.ne.jp/nicepar-kaz90/kaz.osannpo.nikki04/3.htm Along the Sakaigawa II South Course] (Kaz’s Walking Diary).” At this time, Kuraokami, formerly the deity, is thought to have been merged into Okura Inari Shrine; from then on, only Takemikazuchi was treated as the deity of this shrine’s main hall. Later, as noted above, in 1909 (Meiji 42), as part of restoration after the shrine had fallen into ruin without rebuilding following the fire in 1876 (Meiji 9), Takeminakata was merged from Suwa Shrine (a subsidiary shrine in the same area), and a temporary shrine hall was built in the precincts. Further, in 2012 (Heisei 24), Kuraokami—thought to be the original deity—was merged from Okura Inari, making the shrine’s deities three: Kuraokami, Takemikazuchi, and Takeminakata. === Cultural properties in the precincts === {{seealso|List of designated cultural properties of Yamato City}} * Fukami Shrine name marker (Yamato City designated Important {{ill|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860|qid=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}}[http://www.city.yamato.lg.jp/web/kouhou/youranP32-33.html Yamato Catalog – Cultural Properties] (Yamato City)) - Erected in {{ill|lt=1791|Q7784|qid=Q7784|1=1791}} ({{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|qid=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 3). The front reads, “Sagami Province, one of thirteen seats, Fukami Shrine”{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 深見村 鹿島社}}. It is said to have been erected by {{ill|lt=Sakamoto Shigeharu|Q11425381|qid=Q11425381|lt=Sakamoto Naiki Shigeharu|1=Sakamoto Shigeharu}} (''born {{ill|lt=1630|Q6856|qid=Q6856|1=1630}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 7), died {{ill|lt=1693|Q7689|qid=Q7689|1=1693}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 6)''), head of the hatamoto Sakamoto family that ruled Fukami village through the Edo period and served as Commissioner of Temples and Shrines, briefly elevated to daimyō (lord of {{ill|lt=Fukami Domain|Q10863506|qid=Q10863506|1=Fukami Domain}}); however, the erection year above does not match Shigeharu’s active years. * {{ill|lt=Japanese elm|Q844010|qid=Q844010|1=Ulmus davidiana var. japonica}} (Yamato City designated {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|qid=Q23790|1=Natural monument}}[http://www.city.yamato.lg.jp/web/content/000133517.pdf List of designated cultural properties of Yamato City (PDF)] (Yamato City)) - A {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sacred tree|1=UNKNOWN}} with a trunk circumference of 4 m and height of 43 m, said to be 500 years old. Since it normally grows naturally in {{ill|lt=mountainous areas|Q878223|qid=Q878223|1=Highland}}, growth in lowlands is rare nationwide. As its species was once unknown, the shrine has long called it the “{{ill|lt=Nanjamonja|Q11324098|qid=Q11324098|lt=Nanjamonja tree|1=Nanjamonja tree}}.” In the past, on the north side of this tree, there was also a sacred “Engi Pine” said to be over 1,300 years old{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 深見村 鹿島社}}. File:Fukami-jinja-13sya.JPG|Fukami Shrine name marker
(the side says “Auspicious day, 11th month, Kansei 3 Shin-kai year” ({{ill|lt=1791|Q7784|qid=Q7784|1=1791}})[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304140912/http://www.geocities.jp/loveisibotoke/hukami_jinjya.html Stone Buddhas of Yamato City: Fukami Shrine]) File:Fukami-jinja-nanjamonja.JPG|Japanese elm in the precincts
(winter appearance, photographed January 2013) File:Fujami-nanjyamonjya 4.jpg|Same Japanese elm, different season
(late-summer appearance, photographed September 2013) === Access === * About 10–15 minutes on foot east from “{{ill|lt=Yamato Station (Kanagawa)|Q1036368|qid=Q1036368|lt=Yamato Station|1=Yamato Station (Kanagawa)}}” on the {{ill|lt=Sotetsu Main Line|Q846361|qid=Q846361|lt=Sotetsu Line|1=Sōtetsu Main Line}} and {{ill|lt=Odakyu Enoshima Line|Q1063924|qid=Q1063924|lt=Odakyu Line|1=Odakyū Enoshima Line}}. === Other === === Fukami Shrine South Site === Prior to construction of the [https://web.archive.org/web/20040607235323/http://homepage3.nifty.com/c-fukamiminami/index.html Yamato City Community Center Fukami Minami Hall], an {{ill|lt=archaeological excavation|Q959782|qid=Q959782|1=Archaeological excavation}} for record preservation was conducted from April to July {{ill|lt=1988|Q2426|qid=Q2426|1=1988}} (Shōwa 63). This survey discovered one {{ill|lt=pit dwelling|Q959782|qid=Q959782|1=Archaeological excavation}} from the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|qid=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, four pit dwellings and two {{ill|lt=post-built structures|Q1695743|qid=Q1695743|1=Post in ground}} from the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, and two ditch-like {{ill|lt=features|Q814254|qid=Q814254|1=Feature (archaeology)}} from the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|qid=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}. In addition to artifacts from these features, {{ill|lt=stone tools|Q479257|qid=Q479257|1=Stone tool}} from the {{ill|lt=Japanese Paleolithic|UNKNOWN|lt=Paleolithic}} and {{ill|lt=Jōmon period|Q52824|qid=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}} were also found[http://www.city.yamato.lg.jp/web/shakai/annaiban.html Cultural Property Guide Boards in Yamato City] “Fukami Shrine South Site” (the guide board is located southwest of the [https://web.archive.org/web/20040607235323/http://homepage3.nifty.com/c-fukamiminami/index.html Yamato City Community Center Fukami Minami Hall] in 1-chome Fukamidai, Yamato City). ; Pit-dwelling Structure No. 5 Pit-dwelling Structure No. 5 (rectangular plan, 3.7 m × 3.5 m, about 0.3 m deep) is a late- to end-9th-century Heian-period feature. It had been damaged by the ditch feature mentioned above, and {{ill|lt=pillars|Q4817|qid=Q4817|1=Column}} and {{ill|lt=roof|Q83180|qid=Q83180|1=Roof}} did not survive, but it had a {{ill|lt=kamado|Q12177411|qid=Q12177411|1=Kamado}} on the east side and an underfloor {{ill|lt=pit|Q1421012|qid=Q1421012|1=Prehistoric storage pits}} lined with {{ill|lt=clay|Q42302|qid=Q42302|1=Clay}} (purpose unknown; it was filled during use of the building). Many earthenware vessels used as everyday {{ill|lt=household utensils|Q11376286|qid=Q11376286|1=household utensils}} were excavated from inside the building remains. No other remains from the same period were found, but further surveys in surrounding areas are expected to clarify the full picture of the {{ill|lt=settlement|Q486972|qid=Q486972|1=Human settlement}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|refs= [http://www.tesshow.jp/kanagawa/yamatozama/shrine_yamato_fukami.html Overview of Fukami Shrine] (Neko no Ashiato) |2}} === Bibliography === * {{ill|lt=Dai Nihon Chishi Taikei|Q11435652|qid=Q11435652|1=Dai-Nihon Chishi Taikei}} 21, '{{ill|lt=Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudokikō|Q11503942|qid=Q11503942|1=Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudoki-kō}} Vol. 3' ({{ill|lt=Yūzankaku|Q11659206|qid=Q11659206|1=Yuzankaku}}, 1998) * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第38巻新編相模国風土記稿3巻之64村里部高座郡巻之6|chapter=澁谷庄 深見村 鹿島社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/164}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 深見村 鹿島社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-08}} * Fukami Shrine precinct “History” board, official pamphlet 'Engishiki Fukami Shrine History,' Kanagawa Association of Shrines “Fukami Shrine” page, etc. * 'Kanagawa Shrines Guidebook: A Stroll of the Heart' ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanashin Publishing|1=UNKNOWN}} / supervised by Kanagawa Association of Shrines, Oct. 20, 1997) === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province|Q11582137|qid=Q11582137|1=List of Shikinaisha in Sagami Province}} === External links === {{Wikicommons|Category:Fukami-jinja}} *
